# Reporting

Every run writes one markdown report to
`.github/upstream-sync/reports/YYYY-MM-DDTHHmm.md`. The report doubles
as the PR description (success path) and the issue body (stuck path).

## Report template

```markdown
# Upstream sync — <STATUS> — <YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm UTC+TZ>

**Status:** <ok | no-op | stuck | skipped-locked>
**Host:** <hostname>
**Duration:** <Xm Ys>
**Baseline (before run):** `<old_sha>` (<old_sha_short>) — <old_subject>
**Upstream HEAD:**         `<new_sha>` (<new_sha_short>) — <new_subject>

---

## Summary

- Commits picked:                **<N>**
- Revert pairs dropped:          **<P>** (= <2P> commits skipped, net zero)
- Upstream-empty commits skipped:**<E>**
- Tier-0 auto-resolutions:       **<R>** (across <F> files)
- Tier-2 LLM resolutions:        **<L>** (only when -TryTier2)
- Tier-3 escalation (stuck at):  <STUCK_SHA or "none">

## Picked commits (oldest → newest)

| # | SHA | Subject | Author |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | <short> | <subject> | <author> |
| ... | | | |

## Dropped revert pairs

| Original SHA | Original subject | Revert SHA |
|---|---|---|
| <short> | <subject> | <short> |

## Empty / no-op commits skipped

| SHA | Subject |
|---|---|
| <short> | <subject> |

## Tier-0 auto-resolutions

| Commit SHA | File |
|---|---|
| <short> | `.github/workflows/spelling2.yml` |

## (Stuck only) Conflict diagnostics

**Conflicting commit:** [`<sha>`](https://github.com/microsoft/terminal/commit/<sha>) — <subject>
**Author:** <author>
**Files in conflict:**

| Path | Classification | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `<path>` | semantic-overlap | both sides changed `<function>` |
| `<path>` | deleted-by-us | upstream modifies a file we removed |

**Attempted resolutions:**
- Tier 0: <hit/no match for these paths>
- Tier 1: <not applicable / would skip but conflict not resolved>
- Tier 2: <skipped (flag off) / LOW_CONFIDENCE from <agent_id>>

**Pickup branch:** `upstream-sync/<date>` (pushed to origin)

**How to resume:**

1. `git switch upstream-sync/<date>`
2. Resolve the conflict in `<path>`. Reference:
   - Upstream commit: https://github.com/microsoft/terminal/commit/<sha>
   - Fork file at HEAD: `git show HEAD:<path>`
3. `git add <path>` and `git cherry-pick --continue`
4. Push, open a PR titled `chore(upstream-sync): manual resolution for <shortSha>`, merge it.
5. Run:
   ```
   pwsh .github/skills/upstream-sync/scripts/clear-stuck.ps1 -ResolvedThroughSha <sha>
   ```
6. The next scheduled sync resumes from <sha>+1.

---

_Generated by `.github/skills/upstream-sync/scripts/05-write-report.ps1`._
```

## Stuck-issue template

The issue body is the report itself, plus a short header explaining
urgency:

```markdown
🛑 **Upstream sync stopped at a conflict that needs human judgement.**

The scheduler will keep skipping its runs until this issue is resolved
and the stuck-lock is cleared. No alarm — the lock is intentional.

To unblock:
1. Follow "How to resume" in the report below.
2. Close this issue after `clear-stuck.ps1` runs cleanly.

<full report content>
```

Label: `upstream-sync-stuck` (apply via `gh issue create --label`).

## Why always write a report

- The "ok" report becomes the PR description automatically — reviewers
  see what landed and why anything was skipped.
- The "no-op" report proves the scheduler ran (useful for "did it stop
  ticking?" debugging) without polluting issue/PR queues.
- The "stuck" report is the issue body — humans don't need to re-derive
  what was attempted.
- The "skipped-locked" report shows the lock did its job (no duplicate
  destruction).

Retention: keep all reports indefinitely. They're small markdown.
